content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
February 18,
1993
Honorable Robert C. Gustaveson
Los Angeles Superior Court
400 Civic Center Plaza
Pomona,
Ca
91766
RE:
Your Request for Confirmation
of Telephone Advice
Our File No.
A—93-37
Dear Judge Gustaveson:
You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided
to you concerning the conflict of interest disclosure provisions
of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”).
1
!
QUESTION
As a recently retired judge who expects to serve more than 30
days during a 12-month period,
are you required to file a leaving
office statement of economic interests
(Form 721)
or should you
continue filing annual statements?
CONCLUSION
Because you have not filed a leaving office statement for the
Superior Court of Los Angeles County and you expect to serve
30
days in a
12—month period,
you will continue filing annual
statements of economic interests.
Your statement will be filed
with the clerk of the court to which you are assigned.
When your
assignment ends and you do not anticipate receiving a new
assignment,
you must file a leaving office statement within
30
days of leaving your last assignment.
1/
Government Code Sections 81000—91015.
All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000,
q.
All references to regulations are to Title
2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O. Box 807 •
Sacramento, CA
95812 •
(916) 322-5660


Judge Robert C. Gustaveson
Page Two
FACTS
You retired from the Los Angeles County Superior Court on
January 8,
1993.
You have accepted an assignment to sit-as a
retired judge commencing immediately.
This assignment is expected
to last six months.
Based on advice from the clerk of the Los
Angeles Superior Court,
you have not filed a leaving office
statement of economic interests.
ANALYSIS
The Act requires that judges,
including retired judges who
continue to serve on a part—time basis, file statements of
economic interests
(Form 721).
(Section 87200—87204.)
However,
Regulation 187242/ provides that retired judges are not required
to file statements of economic interests unless they serve or
expect to serve 30 days or more in any 12—month period.
Once your assignment ends and you do not receive or
anticipate receiving any new assignments,
you must file a leaving
office-statement of economic interests within 30 days of leaving
your last assignment.
The leaving office statement will be filed
with the clerk of the court of your last assignment.
If you have any questions or need further assistance, please
contact me at
(916)
322—5662.
Sincerely,
Jeff Marschner
General Counsel
‘It
/ /
by:
Linda Moureaux
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance Division
2/
Copies of Commission regulations and opinions are available in
many law libraries.
Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of l0 per page.

Judge Robert C. Gustaveson

Gustaveson Los Angeles Superior Court 400 Civic Center Plaza Pomona, Ca 91766 RE: Your Request for Confirmation of Telephone Advice Our File No. A—93-37 Dear Judge Gustaveson: You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided to you concerning the conflict of interest disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”). 1 ! QUESTION As a recently retired judge who expects to serve more than 30 days during a 12-month period, are you required to file a leaving office statement of economic interests (Form 721) or should you continue filing annual statements? CONCLUSION Because you have not filed a leaving office statement for the Superior Court of Los Angeles County and you expect to serve 30 days in a 12—month period, you will continue filing annual statements of economic interests. Your statement will be filed with the clerk of the court to which you are assigned. When your assignment ends and you do not anticipate receiving a new assignment, you must file a leaving office statement within 30 days of leaving your last assignment. 1/ Government Code Sections 81000—91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, q. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95812 • (916) 322-5660 Judge Robert C. Gustaveson Page Two FACTS You retired from the Los Angeles County Superior Court on January 8, 1993. You have accepted an assignment to sit-as a retired judge commencing immediately. This assignment is expected to last six months. Based on advice from the clerk of the Los Angeles Superior Court, you have not filed a leaving office statement of economic interests. ANALYSIS The Act requires that judges, including retired judges who continue to serve on a part—time basis, file statements of economic interests (Form 721). (Section 87200—87204.) However, Regulation 187242/ provides that retired judges are not required to file statements of economic interests unless they serve or expect to serve 30 days or more in any 12—month period. Once your assignment ends and you do not receive or anticipate receiving any new assignments, you must file a leaving office-statement of economic interests within 30 days of leaving your last assignment. The leaving office statement will be filed with the clerk of the court of your last assignment. If you have any questions or need further assistance, please contact me at (916) 322—5662.
